executive_summary.summary: HoloEngine has a trust-readiness buildout underway for early enterprise design-partner review. The current packet explains target architecture, current gaps, provider questions, evidence collection, and claim boundaries.
executive_summary.mode_boundary: Target production customer mode is separate from HoloVerify benchmark/evidence mode.
executive_summary.provider_posture: OpenAI, xAI, and MiniMax are current benchmark/provider route vendors with retention, DPA, BAA/HIPAA, subprocessor, zero-retention, and data-residency posture marked VERIFY/TBD or TBD.
executive_summary.soc_2_posture: Gap-closure roadmap only; not SOC 2 readiness, certification, audit completion, or auditor validation.
blocked_claim_boundary: customer payloads are never sent to providers

taxonomy.HoloEngine: Core runtime trust layer and primary assurance scope.
taxonomy.HoloGov: Governance, selector, deterministic gate, and final-verdict control layer inside HoloEngine.
taxonomy.HoloVerify: Benchmark and evaluation system for HoloEngine; separate from production customer payload handling.
taxonomy.HoloChat: Connected surface only if deployed as part of a customer-facing production system.
taxonomy.HoloBuild: Connected surface only if deployed as part of a customer-facing production system.
taxonomy.HoloBrain: Conditional only if enabled in deployment.

what_holoengine_is: Core runtime trust layer and primary assurance scope.
Target production role is bounded ALLOW/ESCALATE review.
HoloGov is included as governance, selector, deterministic gate, and final-verdict control layer.
HoloVerify is benchmark/evidence mode and is not production customer payload handling.
what_holoengine_is_not: SOC 2 ready, SOC 2 certified, SOC 2 compliant, or auditor validated
covered by verified DPA or BAA terms with any provider
backed by verified provider zero retention
backed by verified data residency guarantees
proven to have production controls operating effectively
RAM-only, 0 ms retention, or memory-zeroing by design
guaranteed to never send customer payloads to providers
a downstream business-action execution layer by default
a wrapper that automatically brings HoloChat or HoloBuild into scope

target_production_customer_mode.purpose: Bounded review of customer operational requests or evidence packets.
target_production_customer_mode.output: ALLOW or ESCALATE decision response plus metadata.
target_production_customer_mode.provider_bound_content: Minimum necessary bounded prompt content under approved route and provider terms, to be verified.
target_production_customer_mode.retention_posture: Retention, deletion, access controls, and customer evidence-retention mode remain target controls needing evidence.
target_production_customer_mode.claim_boundary: Do not claim implemented production handling until deployment evidence exists.

holoverify_benchmark_evidence_mode.purpose: Benchmark integrity, evaluation, reproducibility, and failure analysis.
holoverify_benchmark_evidence_mode.output: Benchmark results, traces, manifests, scoring maps, raw outputs, and autopsies.
holoverify_benchmark_evidence_mode.provider_bound_content: Frozen benchmark prompts, benchmark packet text, route/role instructions, model-review prompts, and benchmark evidence facts may be used.
holoverify_benchmark_evidence_mode.default_logging_target: Evidence preservation may intentionally include raw outputs and other artifacts.
holoverify_benchmark_evidence_mode.retention_posture: Benchmark artifacts may be preserved for reproducibility and must stay separate from production customer data handling.
holoverify_benchmark_evidence_mode.claim_boundary: Do not use benchmark retention behavior as production privacy proof.

provider_bound_data_in_target_production: role/system instructions for the selected HoloEngine route
bounded customer request text or extracted facts needed for review
operational facts, evidence references, or source snippets needed for evaluation
route, provider, model, retry, latency, token, and error metadata
tenant/request identifiers only where required for routing or audit and only under approved design

current_provider_vendor_posture.current_role: Current benchmark/provider route vendor; may be selected in target production routing only under approved provider/vendor terms.
Current benchmark/provider route vendor; may be selected in target production routing only under approved provider/vendor terms.
Current benchmark/provider route vendor; may be selected in target production routing only under approved provider/vendor terms.

provider_work_to_complete: BAA/HIPAA eligibility review before any healthcare positioning
regional/data-residency review for customer deployment scenarios

soc_2_gap_closure_posture.positioning: Draft SOC 2 readiness/gap assessment exists as a gap-closure roadmap only.
high_priority_gaps: encryption in transit and at rest verification

safe_claims_today: HoloEngine has draft trust architecture artifacts for enterprise trust review.
HoloEngine is the primary trust taxonomy; HoloGov is part of HoloEngine, and HoloChat/HoloBuild are connected surfaces only when included in a deployment.
HoloEngine separates target production customer mode from HoloVerify benchmark/evidence mode in its trust architecture and claim language.
In target production customer mode, HoloEngine is designed to process bounded ALLOW / ESCALATE review requests rather than execute downstream customer business actions by default.
HoloEngine's target production audit-log design is metadata-only by default.
Customer-enabled evidence-retention mode is treated as a separate target mode requiring explicit configuration, retention settings, access controls, and deletion procedures.
Provider/vendor handling is a verification workstream.
OpenAI, xAI, and MiniMax are listed as current benchmark/provider route vendors with key posture fields marked VERIFY/TBD or TBD.
HoloVerify benchmark/evidence mode may preserve artifacts for benchmark integrity and reproducibility.
HoloEngine has identified SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria areas and gaps.
HoloEngine has identified AI governance evidence workstreams.
blocked_claims_today: HoloEngine is SOC 2 ready, SOC 2 certified, SOC 2 compliant, SOC 2 Type I complete, or SOC 2 Type II complete.
HoloEngine is HIPAA ready or HIPAA compliant.
HoloEngine has DPA or BAA coverage with OpenAI, xAI, MiniMax, or all providers.
OpenAI, xAI, or MiniMax provide zero retention for HoloEngine data.
HoloEngine guarantees customer regional processing or data residency.
Customer payloads are never sent to providers.
Customer payloads are never stored by HoloEngine.
HoloEngine production controls are implemented and operating effectively.
HoloEngine has verified TLS 1.3, encryption coverage, or key-management specifics in production.
HoloEngine uses RAM-only processing, 0 ms retention, or memory zeroing.
HoloVerify benchmark/evidence retention is equivalent to production customer data handling.
HoloChat and HoloBuild are automatically covered by HoloEngine trust scope.
HoloEngine executes downstream customer business actions by default.
evidence_collecting_next: Final deployment data-flow diagram and asset inventory.
Authentication, authorization, RBAC, and tenant-isolation evidence.
Encryption in transit and at rest verification.
Runtime metadata-only audit-log samples and redaction tests.
Log storage, access control, retention, and integrity evidence.
Provider terms, DPAs, subprocessors, retention settings, zero-retention eligibility, and regional posture.
Customer evidence-retention mode controls, deletion/export workflow, and support-access boundaries.
Retention/deletion policy, backup handling, and legal hold model.
Incident response plan, severity matrix, notification template, and tabletop evidence.
Secure development lifecycle, vulnerability management, and secrets management evidence.
Selector version/hash logging, provider/model routing logs, trace integrity, no scoring-map leakage tests, and failure autopsy workflow.
Buyer-safe security FAQ, privacy/data-handling FAQ, vendor/subprocessor list, and design-partner pilot terms.

faq.question: Are you SOC 2 certified or SOC 2 ready?
faq.answer: No. HoloEngine has a SOC 2 gap-closure roadmap and evidence plan. It is not claiming SOC 2 readiness, certification, Type I completion, Type II completion, or auditor validation.

faq.question: Is this packet public marketing copy?
faq.answer: No. This packet is for early enterprise design partners and security/privacy/procurement review. It is meant to support diligence and pilot scoping, not public marketing claims.

faq.question: What is HoloEngine?
faq.answer: HoloEngine is the core runtime trust layer that targets bounded ALLOW / ESCALATE review of operational requests, evidence packets, workflow events, or decision-review payloads.

faq.question: Are HoloChat and HoloBuild in scope?
faq.answer: Only if a specific customer-facing deployment includes them. They are connected surfaces, not automatically covered by HoloEngine trust scope.

faq.question: What is HoloVerify?
faq.answer: HoloVerify is the benchmark and evaluation system for HoloEngine. It may preserve benchmark artifacts for reproducibility and failure analysis. It is separate from production customer payload handling.

faq.question: Do you send customer payloads to providers?
faq.answer: The target production design may send minimum necessary bounded prompt content to selected providers when needed for ALLOW / ESCALATE review. HoloEngine does not claim customer payloads are never sent to providers.

faq.question: Are production logs metadata-only?
faq.answer: Metadata-only production audit logging is the target default design. Runtime samples, redaction tests, log storage/access controls, retention settings, and integrity evidence still need to be collected before this can be claimed as implemented production behavior.

faq.question: Do OpenAI, xAI, or MiniMax have zero retention for HoloEngine data?
faq.answer: Not claimed. Retention setting support and zero-retention eligibility are marked VERIFY/TBD and require provider-specific contract and configuration evidence.

faq.question: Do you have DPAs or BAAs with providers?
faq.answer: Not claimed. DPA and BAA/HIPAA posture is currently TBD and requires legal review and provider-specific evidence.

faq.question: Can you guarantee data residency?
faq.answer: Not at this stage. Regional/data-residency posture is TBD and must be reviewed per provider route, infrastructure region, customer requirement, and deployment design.

faq.question: Can a design partner enable evidence retention?
faq.answer: Customer-enabled evidence-retention mode is a target control. It requires explicit configuration, retention settings, access controls, deletion/export procedures, support-access boundaries, and customer documentation before stronger claims can be made.

faq.question: What can we safely rely on today?
faq.answer: You can rely on this packet as a current trust-readiness and gap-closure artifact. It explains the target architecture, mode separation, provider TBDs, blocked claims, and evidence workstreams. It is not a compliance, certification, or operating-effectiveness proof.

review_gate_before_external_use: Legal review for SOC 2, HIPAA, DPA, BAA, provider, retention, residency, and customer-data claims.
Security review for authentication, RBAC, tenant isolation, encryption, logging, monitoring, incident response, and vulnerability claims.
Privacy review for retention, deletion, support access, privacy notice, and evidence-retention claims.
Engineering/HoloGov review for model/provider routing logs, selector version/hash logging, trace integrity, no scoring-map leakage, model change control, and production evidence claims.
